channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Hideki Matsuyama sets the target at Kapalua and leads Collin Morikawa by 1
item.description: KAPALUA, Hawaii (AP) — Collin Morikawa has played Kapalua enough to know that trailing Hideki Matsuyama by seven shots early in the second round was no reason to panic. Sure enough, he nearly caught up to the Japanese star on Friday at The Sentry.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Heat suspend Jimmy Butler for 7 games and will seek to trade him
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — Jimmy Butler was Miami's best player for five years, the leader of a team that went to the NBA Finals twice in his tenure. He talked big and, at times, played bigger. He spoke with reverence for the city, for the franchise, for the famed “Heat Culture.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: College football turns upside down, but this final four is a collection of the sport's big programs
item.description: In the first round of the College Football Playoff, the teams seeded 5-8 all won. In the second round, they all won again.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jets interview former Titans coach Mike Vrabel for their head coaching job
item.description: FLORHAM PARK, N.J. (AP) — The New York Jets interviewed former Tennessee Titans coach Mike Vrabel on Friday for their head coaching vacancy. Vrabel is the second known coaching candidate to meet with the Jets, who announced they completed their interview with him.
item.dc:creator: DENNIS WASZAK Jr., Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Verizon to bring Super Bowl FanFest to life in 30 cities, offering NFL championship hype nationwide
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The Caesars Superdome may be the epicenter for the Super Bowl in New Orleans next month, but Verizon has a game plan to ensure the championship excitement stretches beyond the Big Easy.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN LANDRUM Jr., Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: ESPN doesn't show national anthem, U-S-A chants ahead of Sugar Bowl after New Orleans terror attack
item.description: ESPN is facing criticism after not broadcasting the national anthem prior to the start of the Sugar Bowl game between Georgia and Notre Dame on Thursday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: St. Pete Pier hosts 'Raise the Flags Friday' ahead of Bucs' regular season finale
item.description: The Buccaneers are counting down to the finale of their regular season. Sunday's home game against the New Orleans Saints is a must-win for the team to secure a spot in the playoffs.
item.dc:creator: Beth Rousseau, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Expansion-seeking PWHL ready to drop puck on 9-game neutral site Takeover Tour in Seattle
item.description: Of all the places Hilary Knight has played competitive hockey — from Beijing to Utica, New York — during her 17 U.S. national team seasons, the Boston Fleet captain holds a soft spot for Seattle, not far from her home in Sun Valley, Idaho.
item.dc:creator: JOHN WAWROW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Postponed Sugar Bowl played with enhanced security about 36 hours after terror attack in New Orleans
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Georgia and Notre Dame fans packed a plaza adjacent to the Superdome and enjoyed music under clear skies — under the watch of snipers on rooftops — before filtering into the stadium for Thursday's College Football Playoff quarterfinal at the Sugar Bowl. “It was a lot of fun.
item.dc:creator: BRETT MARTEL, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Caitlin Clark says college transfers should sit out a year amid 'egregious' recruiting tactics
item.description: Caitlin Clark agreed with Travis Kelce that college athletes who transfer should have to sit out a full year before being eligible to play for a new school.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Coby White, Torrey Craig lead the Bulls past the Hornets in overtime, 115-108
item.description: Coby White narrowly missed his first career triple-double, finishing with 23 points, 10 rebounds and nine assists, and Torrey Craig scored 18 with five 3-pointers after halftime as the Chicago Bulls held on to beat the Charlotte Hornets 115-108 in overtime Monday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Blues dominate Blackhawks 6-2 in Discover NHL Winter Classic
item.description: The St. Louis Blues and Chicago Blackhawks will close the 2024 calendar year with a head-to-head battle outdoors: The NHL Discover Winter Classic.
item.dc:creator: Joey Schneider, WGN 9

item.title: Moment of silence for former President Jimmy Carter held before the Falcons-Commanders game
item.description: Jimmy Carter was honored with a moment of silence before the Atlanta Falcons’ game at the Washington Commanders on Sunday night, hours after the 39th president of the United States died at the age of 100 in Plains, Georgia.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Jake Evans scores for the career-high 4th consecutive game, surging Canadiens beat Lightning 5-2
item.description: Jake Evans scored for the career-high fifth consecutive game and the surging Montreal Canadiens beat the Tampa Bay Lightning 5-2 on Sunday night.
item.dc:creator: ERIK ERLENDSSON Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Colorado football fans face a dramatic week across three teams
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX)—Colorado football fans experienced a week filled with high-stakes action, featuring the Denver Broncos chasing a playoff berth and the Colorado Buffaloes and Colorado State Rams battling in bowl games.
item.dc:creator: Titus Cleveland, KREX 5
